title: 第 104 節
en: Afata^atru said to him : ' Do not challenge me on this. I meditate on him as Indra Vaikuz^a, as the unconquerable army. Whoso meditates on him thus, becomes victorious, unconquerable, conquering his enemies.'
1 This is not mentioned in the B/Yhad-ara«yaka.
304 kaush!taki-upanishad.
9. Balaki said: 'The person that is in the fire, on him I meditate.'

A^atasatru said to him : ' Do not challenge me on this. I meditate on him as powerful. Whoso meditates on him thus, becomes powerful among others V 10. Balaki said: 'The person that is in the water, on him I meditate.'

A^atasatru said to him : ' Do not challenge me on this. I meditate on him as the self of the name. Whoso meditates on him thus, becomes the self of the name.' So far with regard to deities (mythological) ; now with regard to the body (physiological).
11. Balaki said: 'The person that is in the mirror, on him I meditate.'

A^atasatru said to him : ' Do not challenge me on this. I meditate on him as the likeness. Whoso meditates on him thus, to him a son is born in his family who is his likeness, not one who is not his likeness.'
12. Balaki said: 'The person that is in the echo, on him I meditate.'

A^atasatru said to him : ' Do not challenge me on this. I meditate on him as the second, who never goes away. Whoso meditates on him thus, he gets a second from his second (his wife), he becomes doubled 2.'
13. Balaki said: 'The sound that follows a man, on that I meditate,'
A^atasatru said to him : ' Do not challenge me on 1 Instead of anyeshu, the second text, as printed by Professor Cowell, has anv esha.
2 This paragraph does not occur in the Br/had-ara;/yaka.

IV ADHYAYA, I 8. 305 this. I meditate on him as life. Whoso meditates on him thus, neither he himself nor his offspring will faint before the time/ 14. Balaki said: 'The person that is in the shadow, on him I meditate.'
A^ata-satru said to him : ' Do not challenge me on this. I meditate on him as death. Whoso meditates on him thus, neither he himself nor his offspring will die before the time.'
15. Balaki said: 'The person that is embodied, on him I meditate.'
A^atasatru said to him : ' Do not challenge me on this. I meditate on him as Lord of creatures. Whoso meditates on him thus, is multiplied in offspring and cattle.'
16. Balaki said : ' The Self which is conscious (pragma), and by whom he who sleeps here, walks about in sleep, on him I meditate.'
A^atai"atru said to him : ' Do not challenge me on this. I meditate on him as Yama the king. Whoso meditates on him thus, everything is subdued for his excellencies.'
17. Balaki said: 'The person that is in the right eye, on him I meditate.'

A^ata^atru said to him : ' Do not challenge me on this. I meditate on him as the self of the name, as the self of fire, as the self of splendour. Whoso meditates on him thus, he becomes the self of these.'
18. Balaki said: 'The person that is in the left eye, on him I meditate.'
A^atasatru said to him : ' Do not challenge me on this. I meditate on him as the self of the true, as the self of lightning, as the self of light. Whoso [3] x 306 KAUSHITAKI-UPANISHAD.
meditates on him thus, he becomes the self of these.'
19. After this Balaki became silent. A^atasatru said to him : ' Thus far only (do you know), O Balaki ? ' ' Thus far only,' replied Balaki.

Then A^ata^atru said to him : ' Vainly did you challenge me, saying: "Shall I tell you Brahman?" O Balaki, he who is the maker of those persons (whom you mentioned), he of whom all this is the work, he alone is to be known.'

Thereupon Balaki came, carrying fuel in his hand, saying : ' May I come to you as a pupil ? ' A^ata.yatru said to him : ' I deem it improper that a Kshatriya should initiate a Brahmawa. Come, I shall make you know clearly.' Then taking him by the hand, he went forth. And the two together came to a person who was asleep. And A^'atai'atru called him, saying : ' Thou great one, clad in white raiment, Soma, King1.' But he remained lying. Then he pushed him with a stick, and he rose at once. Then said A^ata^atru to him : ' Balaki. where did this person here sleep ? Where was he ? Whence came he thus back ? ' Balaki did not know.

20. And A^ata^atru said to him : 'Where this person here slept, where he was, whence he thus came back, is this : The arteries of the heart called Hita< extend from the heart of the person towards the surrounding body. Small as a hair divided a thousand times, they stand full of a thin fluid of various colours, white, black, yellow, red. In these the person is when sleeping he sees no dream.
1 See § 3 init.

IV ADHYAYA, 20. 2>°7 Then he becomes one with that pra;/a alone. Then speech goes to him with all names, the eye with all forms, the ear with all sounds, the mind with all thoughts. And when he awakes, then, as from a burning fire, sparks proceed in all directions, thus from that self the pra/zas (speech, &c.) proceed, each towards its place, from the pra^as the gods, from the gods the worlds. And as a razor might be fitted in a razor-case, or as fire in the fire-place (the aram on the altar), even thus this conscious self enters the self of the body (considers the body as himself) to the very hairs and nails. And the other selfs (such as speech, &c.) follow that self, as his people follow the master of the house. And as the master feeds with his people, nay, as his people feed on the master, thus does this conscious self feed with the other selfs, as a master with his people, and the other selfs follow him, as his people follow the master. So long as Indra did not understand that self, the Asuras conquered him. When he understood it, he conquered the Asuras and obtained the pre-eminence among all gods, sovereignty, supremacy. And thus also he who knows this obtains pre-eminence among all beings, sovereignty, supremacy, — yea, he who knows this.
zh: 阿法塔楚對他說：「不要在這方面向我挑戰。我冥想他為因陀羅‧韋庫茲那（Indra Vaikuz^a），即不可戰勝的軍隊。凡以此方式冥想他的人，便會變得勝利者、不可戰勝者，戰勝其敵人。」
1 這在《白騎梵書奧義書》(Bṛhad-āraṇyaka) 中未見提及。
304 考士塔基奧義書（kaush!taki-upanishad）。
巴剌奇說：「我冥思於火中之人。」
阿塔塔修（Atasatru）對他說：「不要在這事上與我爭執。我冥思他為強大者。凡以此方式冥思他者，在他人中變得強大。」10. 巴拉基說：「那位在水中的人格，我冥思於他。」
阿達薩特魯對他說：「不要在這一點上與我爭論。我冥想他為名字之自我。凡是如此冥想他者，便成為名字之自我。」以上係關於提婆（神祇）者；現在涉及身體（生理學）。
巴拉基說：「我冥想的是鏡子裡面的那個人。」
阿塔薩圖（A^atasatru）對他說：「不要在這點上與我爭論。我冥想他如同形象一般。凡如此冥想他者，其家族中必生一子，其子乃其形象也，非非其形象者。」
巴拉基說：「那位存在於迴響中的人格，我對他進行冥想。」
阿闍世王對他說：「不要在這點上與我爭辯。我冥想他如同第二者，他永不離去。誰如此冥想他，他便從他的第二者（他的妻子）得到第二者，他便成為雙倍的。」
13. 巴拉基說：『追隨人而來之音聲，我於彼冥想，』
阿闍世王對他說：「不要挑戰我。」 --- **注**：此段文本極為簡短且片段性，似乎為校勘註記的一部分。原文本身即不完整（「Do not challenge me on」後無補語），故譯文保持其不完整之狀。若需完整語境，建議查閱原文獲更多上下文。
2 本段文字未見於《布哈達阿蘭亞卡奧義書》(Br/had-ara;/yaka)。
第四阿地雅亞，第一篇，第八章，第305節。我冥想他作為生命。凡如此冥想他者，他自己及其後代都不會在時期未至之前而衰微。 第14節。巴剌基說：「那在陰影中的人格者，我冥想於他。」
阿陀-薩都向他說：「不要在此事上與我爭論。我冥想他為死亡。凡如此冥想他者，他本人及其子孫皆不會在定時之前死亡。」
15. 巴拉基說：「我冥想的是那個具有形體的人。」
阿闍世王對他說：「不要在這件事上與我爭論。我冥想他為眾生之主。凡如此冥想他者，其子嗣與牲畜皆得增殖。」
巴拉基說：「我冥想於那自我（Self），它是有意識的（pragma），並且那睡眠者藉由他而在夢中行走。」
阿阿他依阿特盧（Āatai"atru）對他說：「不要在這上面與我爭論。我冥想他為閻摩王（Yama the king）。誰如此冥想他，一切都為他的卓越品質所臣服。」
17. 巴拉基說：「那住在右眼中的人格，我以之為冥想對象。」
阿陀陀多對他說：「不要在這件事上與我爭辯。我冥想他如同名字之自我，如同火之自我，如同光輝之自我。凡是如此冥想他的人，他就會成為這些事物之自我。」
18. 巴拉基說：「我冥想的是位於左眼中的那個人（the person that is in the left eye）。」
阿塔塔圖說道：「不要在此事上與我爭論。我冥想他為真實之自我、為閃電之自我、為光明之自我。凡是……[3] × 306 《考希塔基奧義書》。
冥想他如斯者，他成為此等物之自我。'
19. 此後，巴拉基陷入沉默。阿塔塔薩圖對他說：「巴拉基啊，你的知識僅止於此乎？」巴拉基答道：「我的知識僅止於此。」
阿闍多（A^ata^atru）於是對他說：「你徒勞地向我挑戰，說：『我應該告訴你梵天嗎？』巴拉基啊，製造那些人物（你所提及的人物）的那位，一切皆為其作為的那位，唯有他才是應當認識的。」

於是婆羅奇（Balaki）來到，手裡拿著柴火，說道：『我可以作為學生來侍奉您嗎？』阿遮多耶樹（A^ata.yatru）對他說：『我認為剎帝利啟蒙婆羅門是不當的。來吧，我將使你清楚地明白。』於是他牽著他的手走了出去。兩人一起來到一個正在睡眠中的人面前。阿遮多耶樹呼喚他，說道：『你偉大的人啊，穿著白色衣袍，蘇摩（Soma），國王啊。』但那人仍然躺著。於是他用木杖推了他一下，他立刻起身了。隨後阿遮多耶樹對婆羅奇說：『婆羅奇，這個人在這裡睡著了，他在哪裡睡眠？他在哪裡？他是從何處這樣回來的？』婆羅奇不知道。
20. 阿闍多羅烏（A^ata^atru）對他說：『此人睡眠時所在之處，其所來之處如下：稱為希多（Hita）的心臟動脈，自人之心臟向周身延伸。此等脈細如毫髮分為千份，充滿各色稀薄液體，白色、黑色、黃色、紅色。人在其中睡眠時，不見夢象。
見第3節首處。

第四奧義書，第二十經2‧207 爾時彼自我與彼生命氣（prana）合一。爾時言語攜萬名而趣向彼，眼攜萬形而趣向彼，耳攜萬聲而趣向彼，心攜萬思而趣向彼。及彼覺醒時，則如自熊熊烈火中飛出火星四散八方，如是自此自我中，生命氣（言語等）各自趣其所處，自生命氣而生諸提婆，自諸提婆而生諸世界。如利刃可納入刀鞘，或如火可納入爐所（祭壇上之阿拉姆），如是此有智之自我進入身體之自我（視身體為己身），及至毛髮爪甲。其他自我（如言語等）隨此自我，如民眾隨家主。如家主與民眾同食，抑或民眾賴家主而食，如是此有智之自我與其他自我同食，如家主與民眾，而其他自我隨之，如民眾隨家主。凡因陀羅未曾理解此自我時，阿修羅軍勝之。及彼既理解，則克勝阿修羅，於諸提婆中獲得優越、主權、至尊。如是知此者亦於諸有情中獲得優越、主權、至尊——誠哉，知此者是也。
